commission_per_trade: None for forex CFDs (spread-only). Share CFDs: US 2c/share ($15 min), UK 0.10% (£10 min)
pros: 11+ Tier-1 regulators worldwide (FCA, ASIC, BaFin, MAS, JFSA, FINMA)
cons: Share CFD commissions ($15 min per US trade)
Average spreads widen outside core hours

competitors.name: Saxo Bank
competitors.regulators: FCA, ASIC, SFC, JFSA, MAS, FINMA

expert_credentials: Senior Market Analyst, 10+ years forex and CFD trading
expert_take: I've been trading on IG's platform for over three years now. What keeps me coming back is the execution speed — 0.014 seconds on average. The proprietary web platform handles complex multi-chart layouts without lagging, and having TradingView integrated directly means I don't need to switch between tools. The only real downside is the inactivity fee, but if you're actively trading, that's irrelevant.

best_for.reason: Low spreads from 0.6 pips, ProRealTime charting, DMA access via L2 Dealer
17,000+ markets across forex, shares, indices, commodities, crypto, and options
11+ Tier-1 regulators, FTSE 100 listed, 50 years operating history

account_opening_steps: Fill in personal details (name, email, country of residence)
Complete the suitability questionnaire (trading experience, financial situation)
Upload identity documents (passport/ID + proof of address)
Fund your account via bank transfer ($0 minimum) or card (~$450 minimum)
Start trading on demo or live account
